feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: im genuinely concerned about the future
entry.content: For 2 thirds of my life. Having a pc was a huge part of my life. Now with inflation, demand price increases and just overall changes to the world structure. Be it ai, cloud computing, surveillance state digital ids etc. I am increasingly worried i will never own a pc again once mine breaks. I stay up at night thinking about this. That i will never own a home. Be priced out of even food that i like. And that if i want a pc its either get rid of my down-payment for a house thats reducing in purcha

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: The Binding of Isaac doesn't have permadeath
entry.content: Permadeath is the loss of all progression, that's what separates it from normal death. The Binding of Isaac has a set of levels, which if you beat, you get a new set of levels. The set of levels could alternatively be interpreted as a level of levels, or a world like from mario. When you die, you only get sent back to your last checkpoint, rather than starting all over. If The Binding of Isaac had permadeath, you would be sent back to the very first set of levels and have to beat all of the set

entry.title: /b/ Random: that "injection of an anti-psychotic into my body" appointment i have scheduled …
entry.content: that "injection of an anti-psychotic into my body" appointment i have scheduled in a couple days can't come soon enough...this is fucked, i am not doing well right now...is this really supposed to be the rest of my life? just taking meds and going to get that injection every month? forever? that can't possibly be true, especially after my parents kick the bucket, at which point i'm sure i'll abandon the whole notion of trying to be functional and go find real drugs...i've been meaning to do that

entry.title: /int/ International: The most spoken language on the streets of Warsaw is now Spanish. Until recently…

I litterally had a super vivid dream of a guy blowing his brains out with a shotgun and he was on his knees and I was right beheind him standing so the pellets would have it my body and I covered my face insticntivly then woke up, and i'm kind of pissed because that made me wake up to early.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Splinter Cell Remake leak
entry.content: >Slated for Q2-Q4 2027, project in a “fragile state right now” >Non lethal choices being expanded upon >Fully linear >Dynamic light shooting with light/dark meter >Snow Drop Engine (Division/Avatar/Outlaws) >Sticky cameras & gas grenades >Manual alarms and destructible enviroments >Pipe slides & zip lines

entry.content: extremely shallow "press X to awesome" game

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >A new report claims Sony caught many publishers, retailers, and business partne…
entry.content: >A new report claims Sony caught many publishers, retailers, and business partners off guard with its decision to stop making physical PlayStation games in 2028. >According to the report, many partners only learned of it after Sony made the announcement public. One executive at a major AAA publisher said >“Despite our seemingly close working relationship with PlayStation, we weren’t informed that this was happening.” “It’s only hitting us now that we may be out of jobs sooner rather than later.”

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: cereal experiment thread
entry.content: I tried chocolate pillows but instead of milk in the bowl with them I used chilled caramel coffee, because I don't have milk I thought it might make something tasty with the coffee caramel and chocolate all together Sadly it was not that tasty, the bitterness of the coffee did not pair well with the chocolatey cereal

entry.content: Real men have projects/hobbies. My current projects are >learning french >learning the recorder >trying to read as many books as I can in 365 days I'm also a writer and taking a hiatus for the 365 days to come back fresh, new, and better read, and then I will write my masterpiece.

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: Scarletverse General #859: Animated Dog Tits Edition
entry.content: 4chan's collective RWBY AU! You're welcome to share your own OCs, lore, and AI generated images with us! Rules: >1. Worship Scarlet >2. Read the Pastebin: https://pastebin.com/8MjgAFYQ >3. Beware trolls, falseflaggers, and edgelords >4. No bitching about other ships >5. Regular RWBY discussion goes to RWBY/RT General >6. Have fun! General Greentexts: https://www.mediafire.com/folder/92 3xfr3g94zeq/SV_Greens GTA's Greentexts: https://www.mediafire.com/folder/k8 fmswyo5vuvl/3-31-26 Tiermakers: htt

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Albums you relate to?
entry.content: The first time I listened to Kendrick Lamar’s-Good Kid M.A.A.D City, I spent half the album looking up at the ceiling, saying “wow” under bated breath, and the other half shaking from the flashbacks of my M.A.A.D city, in which, I was of course, a good kid. Anyone relate to what I’m talking about?

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /slop/ - AI Slop and Image Generation Thread #2958
entry.content: >Maid cleanup Edition Fapping-focused AIslop thread for those who enjoy plapping to AI thread perfection. A thread for prompters, editors, requesters, gooners, AI pornmommies, PLAPpers, Stable Diffusion directors, NovelAI artists—anything and everything AIslop. No limits past site rules; use Catbox galleries and uploads if needed. Keep dumps to a minimum and don't be a derailing cunt, thanks. entry.title: /mu/ Music: What does /mu/ think of Orgcore?
entry.content: I'll be honest, I love this genre. As a late gen xer (born in 1979) this shit really resonates with me. I was also born, and raised in the Gainsville area, so I go to the "fest" every year. Hot Water Music are my favorite band.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Shortwave radio
entry.content: Just bought a XHDATA D219 for 11$, pretty highly reviewed on the Internet for it's price. Obviously I would buy something more expensive if I could but it is what it is. Never used shortwave radio before but I've recently become really fascinated by radio for some reason so I got one. Where do I get started? Do you have any pieces of advice? Also feel free to use this thread for general discussion around shortwave.

entry.title: /b/ Random: if i was technically brain-dead but something else outside of my body was keepin…
entry.content: if i was technically brain-dead but something else outside of my body was keeping me alive, how could i tell? i suspect i may already be dead and might actually be experiencing some form of "psychosomatic reality", as i would call it...a "reality" that technically doesn't exist, but is as good as real to the first-person observer...i also suspect everything i say and do is being recorded somehow

entry.title: /b/ Random: is it possible for someone to do hallucinogens, go on a trip, and become so imme…
entry.content: is it possible for someone to do hallucinogens, go on a trip, and become so immersed in the trip that they actually just become permanently stuck in that new world? how likely is this, and is it something that would even be possible? because i feel as if my whole life up to this point has been some kind of drug trip and i've just been hallucinating this whole time, although i can't prove that.

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: The government says your wages have never been higher. But I have two Big Macs h…
entry.content: The government says your wages have never been higher. But I have two Big Macs here that disagree. In 2005, the minimum wage was $5.15, and a Big Mac was $2.50, which means an hour of work could buy a Big Mac for you and one for your friend. Fast forward to today. The minimum wage is $7.25, which sounds like an improvement. However, the same burger is now almost $6. So one hour of work today buys you just one Big Mac, so your friend has to go. So the next time you hear the government say wages a

entry.title: /g/ Technology: How to Track Random Lights in the Sky
entry.content: I have seen these flying lights in the sky for almost a decade now. They tend to fly in random directions, so they're definitely not stars or airplanes. >maybe they're helicopters I live in an area with no airports. Also, I check flight radar and there were no helicopters in the area. Are these some type of drone? But my question moreso is how can I get a look at these things? I want to buy a long exposure camera so I can record this. What the fuck are these flying lights that move at weird

entry.title: /int/ International: Russian socialism was incredibly conservative after Stalin came to power. Wester…
entry.content: Russian socialism was incredibly conservative after Stalin came to power. Western society was much, much, much, much more liberal in its customs than the Soviet Union. Abortion was prohibited in the USSR, gays were persecuted, and men could beat their wives and the Soviet state would do nothing.
